title: Athyrium fluviale
intro: Athyrium filix-femina, commonly known as Lady Fern or Athyrium fluviale, is a type of fern native to Asia and Europe, characterized by its delicate fronds and slender stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral activities, which may aid in reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Athyrium fluviale include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine and is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, as well as being used in supplements and capsules for its potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Athyrium fluviale is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It belongs to the genus Athyrium and the order Polypodiales. This classification is part of the plant kingdom's system of categorization, which groups organisms based on their shared characteristics and evolutionary relationships. The species Athyrium fluviale is also known by its synonyms, which may include other names used to identify this particular fern. The exact classification of Athyrium fluviale may vary depending on the source or specific taxonomic classification system used.
